Alternate_Title.Grok: Information Technology Security Analyst (IT Security Analyst)

Description.ChatGPT: Receive and analyze network alerts from various sources within the enterprise and determine possible causes of such alerts
Description.DeepSeek: Monitor network traffic and security alerts to identify potential security incidents
Description.Gemini: Receive and analyze network alerts from various sources (IDS, SIEM, etc.) and determine the possible causes of such alerts.
Description.Grok: Perform analysis of log files from a variety of sources (e.g., individual host logs, network traffic logs, firewall logs, and intrusion detection system [IDS] logs) to identify possible threats to network security.

Description.ChatGPT: Use cyber defense tools for continual monitoring and analysis of system activity to identify malicious activity
Description.DeepSeek: Analyze network traffic and log data to identify anomalous activity
Description.Gemini: Monitor and analyze Intrusion Detection System (IDS) to identify security issues for transmission and solution by the appropriate team.
Description.Grok: Receive and analyze network alerts from various sources within the enterprise and determine possible causes of such alerts.

Description.ChatGPT: Provide timely detection and alerting of possible attacks and anomalous activities
Description.DeepSeek: Perform initial triage of security alerts to determine severity and priority
Description.Gemini: Review logs to identify evidence of past intrusions.
Description.Grok: Perform cybersecurity defense incident triage, to include determining scope, urgency, and potential impact; identifying the specific vulnerability; and make recommendations that enable expeditious remediation.

Description.ChatGPT: Perform event correlation using information gathered from multiple enterprise sources
Description.DeepSeek: Document and escalate confirmed security incidents according to procedures
Description.Gemini: Analyze log files from a variety of sources (e.g., individual host logs, network traffic logs, firewall logs, and intrusion detection system [IDS] logs) to identify possible threats to network security.
Description.Grok: Track and document cybersecurity defense incidents from initial detection through final resolution.

Description.ChatGPT: Validate intrusion detection system alerts against network traffic
Description.DeepSeek: Coordinate with incident response teams during security events
Description.Gemini: Identify and report any anomalies in network, system, or security behavior.
Description.Grok: Perform real-time cybersecurity defense incident handling (e.g., forensic collections, intrusion correlation and tracking, threat analysis, and direct system remediation) tasks to support deployable Incident Response Teams (IRTs).

Description.ChatGPT: Identify and analyze anomalies in network traffic using metadata
Description.DeepSeek: Correlate data from multiple security sources to identify patterns
Description.Gemini: Analyze network traffic to identify anomalous indicators and potential security breaches.
Description.Grok: Correlate incident data to identify specific vulnerabilities and make recommendations that enable expeditious remediation.

Description.ChatGPT: Identify potential threats to network resources
Description.DeepSeek: Respond to security incidents following established procedures
Description.Gemini: Investigate security incidents to determine the extent of the threat and to document remediation actions.
Description.Grok: Monitor the security of digital information.

Description.ChatGPT: Document cybersecurity incidents
Description.DeepSeek: Collect and preserve evidence for security incidents
Description.Gemini: Monitor their organization's networks for security breaches and investigate when one occurs.
Description.Grok: Implement security measures for computer or information systems.

Description.ChatGPT: Document and escalate incidents including event history and impact
Description.DeepSeek: Document incident details, actions taken, and lessons learned
Description.Gemini: Prepare reports that document security breaches and the extent of the damage caused by the breaches.
Description.Grok: Troubleshoot issues with computer applications or systems.

Description.ChatGPT: Provide daily summary reports of network events and activity relevant to cyber defense
Description.DeepSeek: Analyze incident data to determine root cause and impact
Description.Gemini: [Summarised] Real-time monitoring of security consoles and initial triage of security alerts.
Description.Grok: Monitor SIEM systems and security dashboards for unusual activity.

Description.ChatGPT: Monitor computer networks for security breaches and investigate violations
Description.DeepSeek: [summarised] Monitor computer networks for security issues and investigate security breaches
Description.Gemini: [Summarised] Documentation of triage steps and preliminary analysis in the ticketing system.
Description.Grok: Perform initial alert triage: assess alerts for authenticity, severity, and legitimacy; filter false positives.

Description.ChatGPT: Analyze security alerts and determine appropriate response actions
Description.DeepSeek: [summarised] Prepare reports documenting security breaches and the extent of damage
Description.Gemini: [Summarised] Verification of alert legitimacy (True Positive vs. False Positive) before escalation.
Description.Grok: Follow established SOC incident response standard operating procedures to evaluate and respond to common security events.

Description.ChatGPT: Document security incidents and response actions
Description.DeepSeek: [summarised] Perform vulnerability scanning and review scan results
Description.Gemini: [Summarised] Escalating confirmed security incidents to Tier 2 or Tier 3 investigators.
Description.Grok: Document every security event, including findings and actions taken.

Description.ChatGPT: Assess system vulnerabilities for security risks
Description.DeepSeek: [summarised] Monitor security alert systems and respond to alerts
Description.Gemini: [Summarised] Reviewing and validating basic vulnerability scanner results.
Description.Grok: Escalate complex cases to Tier 2 analysts.

Description.ChatGPT: Monitor SIEM dashboards and security tools for suspicious activity
Description.DeepSeek: [summarised] Maintain documentation of security procedures and incident response actions
Description.Gemini: [Summarised] Incident classification and initial impact assessment based on predefined playbooks.
Description.Grok: Collect raw data, review alarms and alerts, assess criticality, and enrich alerts with relevant data.

Description.ChatGPT: Triage alerts and determine whether activity represents a true security incident
Description.DeepSeek: [summarised] Analyze security logs and network traffic patterns
Description.Gemini: [Summarised] Initial log collection and evidence preservation for escalated cases.
Description.Grok: Identify whether an alert is justified or a false positive; identify other high-risk events and potential incidents.

Maintain situational awareness of emerging threats and vulnerabilities

Description.DeepSeek: Ongoing observation of security tools, dashboards, and data sources to detect potential security events

Rule_Description.DeepSeek: Allowed verbs: monitor, observe, watch, track. Typical objects: SIEM, dashboards, network traffic, logs, alerts. Exclusion boundary: does not include analysis beyond initial observation or response actions.

Description.DeepSeek: Initial assessment of security alerts to determine validity, severity, and required response actions

Rule_Description.DeepSeek: Allowed verbs: triage, analyze, investigate, review, classify, enrich. Typical objects: alerts, security events, logs, network traffic, threat intelligence. Exclusion boundary: does not include advanced forensic analysis or malware reverse engineering.

Description.DeepSeek: Execution of predefined response procedures to contain and address confirmed security incidents

Rule_Description.DeepSeek: Allowed verbs: respond, contain, execute, follow, apply, disable, isolate. Typical objects: incidents, playbooks, SOPs, user accounts, endpoints. Exclusion boundary: does not include eradication beyond basic containment or post-incident root cause analysis.

Description.DeepSeek: Recording investigation findings, incident details, and actions taken for knowledge transfer and audit trails

Rule_Description.DeepSeek: Allowed verbs: document, record, report, update, prepare. Typical objects: findings, incidents, tickets, reports, lessons learned. Exclusion boundary: does not include executive-level reporting or strategic documentation.

Description.DeepSeek: Coordination with other analysts, teams, and shifts to ensure continuity of operations

Rule_Description.DeepSeek: Allowed verbs: collaborate, coordinate, handover, participate, share. Typical objects: Tier 2, Tier 3, teams, shifts, knowledge. Exclusion boundary: does not include incident command leadership or cross-organizational coordination beyond SOC.

Description.ChatGPT: Continuous observation of systems and security telemetry to identify suspicious activity
Description.Grok: Continuous observation of SIEM, dashboards, logs, and security tools for unusual activity or potential threats.

Rule_Description.ChatGPT: Tasks involving monitoring logs, SIEM dashboards, or system activity streams
Rule_Description.Grok: Allowed verbs: monitor, observe, review. Typical objects: SIEM events, alerts, logs, dashboards. Exclusion: deep investigation, remediation, or policy creation. Example: TSK-010, TSK-007

Description.ChatGPT: Initial investigation and classification of security alerts
Description.Grok: Assessing alerts for legitimacy, severity, criticality, false positives; enriching with context and determining scope/impact.

Rule_Description.ChatGPT: Tasks analyzing alerts, validating IDS signals, or correlating events to determine threat validity
Rule_Description.Grok: Allowed verbs: assess, triage, filter, identify (false positive/high-risk). Typical objects: alerts, alarms, raw data. Exclusion: full incident investigation or remediation. Example: TSK-011, TSK-003, TSK-016

Description.ChatGPT: Recognition of malicious indicators or anomalous patterns
Description.Grok: Following SOPs/playbooks for initial response, correlation, documentation, and basic handling; escalation of complex cases.

Rule_Description.ChatGPT: Tasks identifying malicious indicators or anomalous network behavior
Rule_Description.Grok: Allowed verbs: respond, document, escalate, correlate (basic). Typical objects: security events, incidents. Exclusion: advanced forensics or root-cause remediation. Example: TSK-012, TSK-004, TSK-014

Description.ChatGPT: Recording security events and producing incident documentation
Description.Grok: Performing initial log/alert analysis, troubleshooting security-related system issues, and basic correlation to support triage.

Rule_Description.ChatGPT: Tasks documenting incidents or preparing SOC reports
Rule_Description.Grok: Allowed verbs: analyze (basic), troubleshoot. Typical objects: logs, alerts, system issues. Exclusion: malware reverse engineering or advanced threat hunting. Example: TSK-001, TSK-002, TSK-009

Description.ChatGPT: Escalating confirmed or complex incidents to higher response tiers

Rule_Description.ChatGPT: Tasks escalating incidents to Tier-2 or incident response teams

Description.ChatGPT: Review of vulnerability data relevant to security monitoring

Rule_Description.ChatGPT: Tasks reviewing vulnerability scan results or assessing system exposure

Description.Gemini: Continuous observation of security systems to identify potential threats.

Rule_Description.Gemini: Verbs: Monitor, Receive, Review, Observe; Objects: Logs, Alerts, SIEM events, Traffic. Exclusion: Deep forensic analysis.

Description.Gemini: Initial assessment of alerts to determine legitimacy and impact.

Rule_Description.Gemini: Verbs: Verify, Validate, Classify, Triage; Objects: Anomalies, False positives, Playbooks. Exclusion: Final remediation.

Description.Gemini: Executing preliminary response actions and transferring data to higher tiers.

Rule_Description.Gemini: Verbs: Escalate, Transmit, Investigate (Initial), Preserve; Objects: Incidents, Tickets, Evidence. Exclusion: Long-term containment.

Description.Gemini: Formal recording of triage findings and security breach details.

Rule_Description.Gemini: Verbs: Document, Prepare, Report, Record; Objects: Reports, Ticketing systems. Exclusion: Compliance auditing.

Rule_Justification.ChatGPT: Task involves analyzing alerts to determine causes
Rule_Justification.DeepSeek: Task involves monitoring network traffic and security alerts, matching THEME-01 allowed verbs and objects.
Rule_Justification.Gemini: Focuses on the 'receive' and 'determine cause' aspect of monitoring.
Rule_Justification.Grok: Basic log file analysis from multiple sources aligns with Detect function and initial analysis rule in THM-004.

Rule_Justification.ChatGPT: Task monitors system activity using cyber defense tools
Rule_Justification.DeepSeek: Task involves analyzing network traffic and log data to identify anomalies, matching THEME-02 investigation domain.
Rule_Justification.Grok: Receiving and determining causes of network alerts is basic analysis supporting triage (Detect).

Rule_Justification.ChatGPT: Task identifies anomalous or malicious activities
Rule_Justification.DeepSeek: Task involves initial triage of security alerts to determine severity and priority, core function of THEME-02.
Rule_Justification.Grok: Explicit triage including scope, urgency, impact matches THM-002 Triage & Assessment (Vielberth Tier 1).

Rule_Justification.ChatGPT: Task correlates events across enterprise telemetry sources
Rule_Justification.DeepSeek: Task involves documenting and escalating confirmed security incidents, matching THEME-04 documentation function.
Rule_Justification.Gemini: Passive analysis of various log sources for threat identification.
Rule_Justification.Grok: Tracking and documenting incidents from detection to resolution fits Respond phase basic handling.

Rule_Justification.ChatGPT: Task validates intrusion detection alerts against network traffic
Rule_Justification.DeepSeek: Task involves coordinating with incident response teams during security events, matching THEME-05 collaboration domain.
Rule_Justification.Gemini: Identification of deviations from normal system behavior.
Rule_Justification.Grok: Real-time handling and correlation tasks align with initial response and SOP execution in THM-003.

Rule_Justification.ChatGPT: Task analyzes anomalies in network traffic metadata
Rule_Justification.DeepSeek: Task involves correlating data from multiple security sources to identify patterns, part of investigation theme.
Rule_Justification.Gemini: Initial monitoring of network traffic for anomalies.
Rule_Justification.Grok: Correlating data for remediation recommendations is basic Respond-level correlation.

Rule_Justification.ChatGPT: Task identifies threats to network resources
Rule_Justification.DeepSeek: Task involves responding to security incidents following established procedures, core function of THEME-03.
Rule_Justification.Gemini: Determining the extent of a threat per NIST IR lifecycle.
Rule_Justification.Grok: Direct monitoring of digital information security matches Alert Monitoring (Detect).

Rule_Justification.ChatGPT: Task records cybersecurity incidents
Rule_Justification.DeepSeek: Task involves collecting and preserving evidence for security incidents, part of incident response containment.
Rule_Justification.Grok: Initial implementation of measures during response aligns with basic handling (though borderline; kept as per source).

Rule_Justification.ChatGPT: Task escalates documented incidents to response teams
Rule_Justification.DeepSeek: Task involves documenting incident details, actions taken, and lessons learned, matching THEME-04.
Rule_Justification.Gemini: Formal documentation of the damage and extent of a breach.
Rule_Justification.Grok: Troubleshooting security-related system issues fits basic analysis rule.

Rule_Justification.ChatGPT: Task prepares daily cyber defense activity summaries
Rule_Justification.DeepSeek: Task involves analyzing incident data to determine root cause and impact, aligns with THEME-02 investigation.
Rule_Justification.Grok: Explicit monitoring of SIEM/dashboards for unusual activity (Vielberth/Radiant/Wiz).

Rule_Justification.ChatGPT: Task monitors networks for security breaches
Rule_Justification.DeepSeek: Task involves monitoring computer networks for security issues and investigating breaches, monitoring aspect matches THEME-01.
Rule_Justification.Gemini: Recording specific triage steps in a system of record.
Rule_Justification.Grok: Core Tier 1 triage: assess authenticity, severity, filter false positives (multiple industry + Vielberth).

Rule_Justification.ChatGPT: Task analyzes alerts to determine response
Rule_Justification.DeepSeek: Task involves preparing reports documenting security breaches and the extent of damage, matching THEME-04 reporting function.
Rule_Justification.Gemini: The explicit 'True Positive vs. False Positive' validation rule.
Rule_Justification.Grok: Following SOC IR procedures for common events matches Respond handling.

Rule_Justification.ChatGPT: Task documents security incidents and responses
Rule_Justification.DeepSeek: Task involves performing vulnerability scanning and reviewing scan results, aligns with investigation theme as initial assessment.
Rule_Justification.Gemini: Direct transfer of confirmed incidents to Tier 2/3.
Rule_Justification.Grok: Documenting findings and actions is standard in incident handling.

Rule_Justification.ChatGPT: Task evaluates system vulnerabilities
Rule_Justification.DeepSeek: Task involves monitoring security alert systems and responding to alerts, monitoring aspect matches THEME-01.
Rule_Justification.Gemini: Initial assessment of scanner output to validate relevance.
Rule_Justification.Grok: Escalation of complex cases is explicit Tier 1 boundary in Respond phase.

Rule_Justification.ChatGPT: Task monitors SIEM dashboards
Rule_Justification.DeepSeek: Task involves maintaining documentation of security procedures and incident response actions, matching THEME-04.
Rule_Justification.Gemini: Use of playbooks to categorize events.
Rule_Justification.Grok: Collecting raw data, reviewing alerts, assessing criticality, enriching data (Q-Sec / Vielberth Tier 1).

Rule_Justification.ChatGPT: Task triages alerts to determine incident validity
Rule_Justification.DeepSeek: Task involves analyzing security logs and network traffic patterns, core investigation function.
Rule_Justification.Grok: Explicit identification of justified alerts vs false positives and high-risk events (Vielberth 2020).

Rule_Justification.ChatGPT: Task escalates confirmed incidents to Tier-2
Rule_Justification.DeepSeek: Task involves monitoring SIEM dashboards and security alerts, direct match to THEME-01.

Rule_Justification.ChatGPT: Task reviews vulnerability scan outputs
Rule_Justification.DeepSeek: Task involves performing first-level triage of security events to determine if they are false positives, core THEME-02 function.

Rule_Justification.ChatGPT: Task performs initial investigation before escalation
Rule_Justification.DeepSeek: Task involves documenting investigation findings and escalating confirmed incidents, matching THEME-04 documentation.

Rule_Justification.ChatGPT: Task investigates suspicious events in SOC monitoring tools
Rule_Justification.DeepSeek: Task involves reviewing vulnerability scan reports and prioritizing remediation, aligns with investigation theme.
Task involves executing basic containment actions such as disabling user accounts or isolating endpoints, core function of THEME-03.
Task involves following standard operating procedures for incident response, matches THEME-03.
Task involves participating in shift handover and knowledge transfer, direct match to THEME-05.
Task involves updating ticket systems with investigation progress and resolution, matching THEME-04 documentation function.
Task involves reviewing and classifying security alerts based on severity, core THEME-02 function.
Task involves performing initial investigation using available security tools, aligns with THEME-02 investigation.
Task involves documenting all actions taken during incident handling, matching THEME-04.
Task involves performing enrichment of alerts using threat intelligence and internal data sources, part of investigation theme.
Task involves identifying false positives and suggesting tuning improvements, aligns with THEME-02 triage function.
Task involves maintaining situational awareness of emerging threats and vulnerabilities, aligns with monitoring theme's contextual function.
Task involves collaborating with Tier 2 and Tier 3 analysts on complex investigations, direct match to THEME-05.
Task involves ensuring compliance with service level agreements for alert response times, aligns with investigation theme's operational discipline.
Task involves performing continuous monitoring of security events, core THEME-01 function.
Task involves applying predefined response playbooks to common incident types, matches THEME-03.

Definition.DeepSeek: The average time elapsed between the occurrence of a security incident and its detection by the SOC analyst or monitoring tools
Definition.Gemini: Count of incidents detected over a rolling period, categorized by severity (Low, Medium, High, Critical).
Definition.Grok: Average time from threat occurrence to detection by the SOC (distinguished from analysis time per Vielberth).

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Sum of (detection time - incident occurrence time) / number of incidents
Formula_Elements.Grok: [Time of detection] – [Time of occurrence] / Number of incidents

Primary_Sources.DeepSeek: NIST SP 800-61 Rev.2, Splunk SOC KPI guidance, Vahteri (2025), Forsberg & Frantti (2023), SANS SOC Survey (2019)
Primary_Sources.Grok: NIST SP 800-61 Rev.2; Splunk; Vahteri (2025) [invalidated]; Forsberg & Frantti (2023)

Definition.DeepSeek: The average time between alert generation and analyst acknowledgement, assignment, or initial engagement with the alert
Definition.Gemini: Qualitative assessment of the depth, accuracy, and completeness of an analyst's triage findings.
Definition.Grok: Average time to fully resolve an incident once detected.

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Sum of (acknowledgement time - alert generation time) / number of alerts
Formula_Elements.Gemini: 7-dimension rubric (Accuracy, Clarity, etc.) via AHP weights
Formula_Elements.Grok: [Time of resolution] – [Time of detection] / Number of incidents

Primary_Sources.DeepSeek: Radiant Security SOC metrics, Vahteri (2025), Agyepong et al. (2023)
Primary_Sources.Grok: NIST SP 800-61 Rev.2; Splunk; Vahteri (2025) [validated]; Forsberg & Frantti (2023)

Definition.DeepSeek: The average time from alert generation to initial triage completion and classification of severity
Definition.Gemini: Average time taken for an analyst to claim an unassigned alert and begin the triage process.
Definition.Grok: Average time from alert generation to analyst acknowledgment.

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Sum of triage completion times / number of alerts
Formula_Elements.Gemini: Sum(Time Acknowledged - Time Alerted) / Total Alerts
Formula_Elements.Grok: [Time of acknowledgment] – [Time of alert] / Number of alerts

Definition.DeepSeek: The average time from incident detection to initiation of response actions or containment
Definition.Gemini: Average duration from alert assignment/acknowledgment to initial classification (True Positive vs. False Positive).
Definition.Grok: Average time to perform initial triage on alerts (Tier 1 core activity).

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Sum of response initiation times / number of incidents
Formula_Elements.Gemini: Sum(Triage End - Triage Start) / Total Alerts
Formula_Elements.Grok: [Time triage complete] – [Time alert received] / Number of alerts

Primary_Sources.DeepSeek: NIST SP 800-61 Rev.2, Splunk SOC KPI guidance, Vahteri (2025)

Definition.DeepSeek: The average time from incident detection to successful containment of the threat (e.g., isolation, blocking)
Definition.Gemini: Average time delta between a Tier 1 analyst validating an incident and transferring it to Tier 2/3.
Definition.Grok: Average time from detection to containment/eradication of threat.

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Sum of containment times / number of incidents
Formula_Elements.Gemini: Sum(Time Escalated - Time Validated) / Total Escalations
Formula_Elements.Grok: [Time containment achieved] – [Time of detection] / Number of incidents

Primary_Sources.DeepSeek: Splunk SOC KPI guidance, Vahteri (2025), Forsberg & Frantti (2023)

Definition.DeepSeek: The average time from incident detection to full resolution, eradication, and incident closure
Definition.Gemini: Percentage of total alerts handled that are determined to be non-threatening or 'noise'.
Definition.Grok: Percentage of alerts investigated that turn out to be benign.

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Sum of resolution times / number of incidents

Primary_Sources.DeepSeek: NIST SP 800-61 Rev.2, Vielberth et al. (2020), Forsberg & Frantti (2023)
Primary_Sources.Grok: Splunk; Vahteri (2025) [validated]; Vielberth et al. (2020); Forsberg & Frantti (2023)

Definition.DeepSeek: The average time from incident detection or alert assignment to escalation to Tier 2 or higher
Definition.Gemini: Percentage of incidents correctly escalated to higher tiers versus those returned as 'False Positives' or 'No Action Needed'.
Definition.Grok: Composite score based on 7 quality dimensions for analyst work (SOC-AAM).

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Sum of escalation times / number of escalated incidents

Definition.DeepSeek: The average time from incident detection to the first analyst action taken on the incident
Definition.Gemini: Qualitative evaluation of the clarity and actionable intelligence provided in an analyst's incident write-up.
Definition.Grok: Proportion of incidents/alerts escalated to higher tiers.

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Sum of reaction times / number of incidents
Formula_Elements.Gemini: Weighted scoring based on predefined reporting standards

Definition.DeepSeek: The average time from vulnerability discovery to successful remediation or patching
Definition.Gemini: Ratio of total security alerts analyzed by the analyst versus the total volume of alerts generated by the system.
Definition.Grok: Volume of alerts processed by analysts (especially Tier 1).

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Sum of remediation times / number of vulnerabilities remediated
Formula_Elements.Grok: Count of alerts per time period

Definition.DeepSeek: The average time elapsed between consecutive security incidents, indicating preventive control effectiveness
Definition.Gemini: Percentage of response actions or playbooks executed automatically versus manual analyst intervention.
Definition.Grok: Alerts handled per analyst per shift / triage efficiency.

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Total time period / (number of incidents - 1)
Formula_Elements.Grok: Alerts handled per analyst per shift

Name.Grok: Number of Critical Incidents Detected (by severity)

Definition.DeepSeek: The time spent on specific tasks such as ticket creation, investigation, or documentation
Definition.Gemini: Measurement of analyst cognitive load and turnover risk based on overbooked time and shift pressure.
Definition.Grok: Count of critical/high/medium/low incidents detected over rolling period (Monitoring & Detection Function).

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Time tracking per task type
Formula_Elements.Gemini: Percentage of time spent over capacity threshold

Definition.DeepSeek: The percentage of incidents or alerts handled within defined Service Level Agreement timeframes
Definition.Gemini: The average time delta between an incident occurring and the SOC discovering it.
Definition.Grok: Count of critical/high/medium/low/zero-day alerts analysed (Analysis Function).

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: (Incidents handled within SLA / total incidents) × 100
Formula_Elements.Gemini: Sum(Detection Time - Incident Start Time) / Total Incidents

Primary_Sources.DeepSeek: Vielberth et al. (2020), Agyepong et al. (2023)

Definition.DeepSeek: The number or percentage of incidents where the reaction SLA was violated
Definition.Gemini: Average time from incident detection to complete containment, eradication, and recovery.
Definition.Grok: Count of misconfigurations identified over rolling period.

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Count or percentage of incidents exceeding reaction SLA threshold
Formula_Elements.Gemini: Sum(Resolution Time - Detection Time) / Total Incidents

Definition.DeepSeek: The number or percentage of incidents where the resolution SLA was violated
Definition.Gemini: Ratio of threats identified post-incident versus total threats intended to be blocked by active controls.
Definition.Grok: Count of vulnerabilities discovered, patches applied, rolled back, overdue (high/critical).

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Count or percentage of incidents exceeding resolution SLA threshold

Definition.DeepSeek: The proportion of security alerts that are determined to be false positives after investigation
Definition.Gemini: Monetary cost of a security breach, including labor, infrastructure damage, and brand loss.
Definition.Grok: Count of incidents handled via automation/playbooks.

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: (Number of false positive alerts / total number of alerts) × 100

Primary_Sources.DeepSeek: Splunk SOC KPI guidance, Forsberg & Frantti (2023), Vahteri (2025), Agyepong (2023)

Definition.DeepSeek: The proportion of security incidents that were not detected by SOC monitoring tools or processes (missed threats)
Definition.Gemini: Evaluation of the quality and completeness of alerts issued by the SIEM to analysts.
Definition.Grok: Count of false positives reported over rolling period (Response Function).

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: (Number of undetected incidents / total number of incidents) × 100
Formula_Elements.Gemini: Scoring based on alert fidelity and actionable data fields

Definition.DeepSeek: The proportion of alerts that are correctly identified as legitimate security incidents
Definition.Gemini: The degree to which the SOC's services meet the needs and expectations of internal/external stakeholders.
Definition.Grok: Qualitative score of incident report accuracy, depth, and completeness.

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: (Number of true positive alerts / total number of alerts) × 100

Definition.DeepSeek: The proportion of incidents escalated from Tier 1 to Tier 2 or higher for further investigation
Definition.Grok: Thoroughness of threat eradication and enterprise-wide IOC sweeping.

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: (Number of escalated incidents / total number of incidents) × 100

Primary_Sources.DeepSeek: Splunk SOC KPI guidance, Vahteri (2025), Agyepong (2023)

Definition.DeepSeek: The proportion of escalated incidents that were correctly identified as requiring higher-tier intervention
Definition.Grok: Percentage of incidents successfully attributed using threat intelligence.

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: (Correctly escalated incidents / total escalated incidents) × 100

Definition.DeepSeek: The degree to which analysts correctly assign severity levels to security alerts
Definition.Grok: Percentage of assets successfully monitored or scanned.

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: (Number of correctly prioritized alerts / total number of alerts) × 100

Definition.DeepSeek: A multi-dimensional measure of analyst performance including completeness, accuracy, timeliness, clarity, relevance, traceability, and consistency of investigation documentation
Definition.Grok: Monetary cost per incident (direct + indirect).

Definition.DeepSeek: A qualitative measure of the completeness, accuracy, and professionalism of incident documentation and reporting

Definition.DeepSeek: The thoroughness of threat eradication ensuring no recurrence of the compromise

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Percentage of incidents with no recurrence within 30 days

Definition.DeepSeek: The proportion of incidents that recur within a defined period due to incomplete remediation

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: (Number of recurring incidents / total incidents) × 100

Definition.DeepSeek: The proportion of confirmed threats that were not detected by SOC monitoring tools or processes

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: (Number of undetected threats / total confirmed threats) × 100

Definition.DeepSeek: The SOC's ability to successfully use threat intelligence to attribute an attack to a specific threat actor or group

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: (Number of incidents with successful attribution / total incidents investigated) × 100

Definition.DeepSeek: The percentage of monitored assets or data sources with active detection coverage

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: (Covered assets / total monitored assets) × 100

Definition.DeepSeek: The percentage of organizational assets that are regularly included in vulnerability scanning activities

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: (Assets scanned regularly / total assets) × 100

Definition.DeepSeek: The total count of assets, endpoints, or devices under active security monitoring

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Count of assets with monitoring agents or log sources

Definition.DeepSeek: The total number of security alerts generated and processed by the SOC over a defined period

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Count of alerts per day/week/month

Definition.DeepSeek: The total number of confirmed security incidents handled by the SOC over a defined period

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Count of incidents per day/week/month

Definition.DeepSeek: The number of incidents handled during a specific shift period

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Count of incidents per shift

Definition.DeepSeek: The number of alerts or incidents assigned to an individual analyst over a defined period

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Count of assigned alerts per analyst per shift/day

Definition.DeepSeek: The number of alerts processed or incidents resolved per analyst per unit time

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Total processed items / analyst hours worked

Definition.DeepSeek: The number of alerts processed per analyst per hour

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Alerts processed / analyst hours worked

Definition.DeepSeek: The count of incidents categorized by severity levels (Critical, High, Medium, Low)

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Count per severity level: Critical, High, Medium, Low

Definition.DeepSeek: The count of critical severity incidents detected over a rolling period

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Count of critical severity incidents

Name.DeepSeek: Number of Alerts Analyzed by Priority

Definition.DeepSeek: The count of alerts analyzed, segmented by priority level (Critical, High, Medium, Low)

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Count per priority level

Definition.DeepSeek: The total count of vulnerabilities identified through scanning or discovery activities

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Count of discovered vulnerabilities

Definition.DeepSeek: The count of security patches successfully applied to vulnerable systems

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Count of patches applied

Definition.DeepSeek: The count of security patches that were rolled back due to issues or incompatibility

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Count of patches rolled back

Definition.DeepSeek: The count of new detection use cases or alert rules developed and deployed

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Count of use cases created

Definition.DeepSeek: The count of Indicators of Compromise successfully implemented into detection tools

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Count of IOCs implemented

Definition.DeepSeek: The count of Indicators of Compromise shared with external partners or information sharing communities

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Count of IOCs shared

Definition.DeepSeek: The count of security misconfigurations identified over a rolling period

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Count of detected misconfigurations

Name.DeepSeek: Percentage of Threats Responded to Manually

Definition.DeepSeek: The proportion of threats that required manual intervention rather than automated response

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: (Threats responded manually / total threats) × 100

Name.DeepSeek: Percentage of Threats Responded to Through Automation

Definition.DeepSeek: The proportion of threats that were handled through automated processes or playbooks

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: (Threats handled by automation / total threats) × 100

Definition.DeepSeek: The count of incidents that were fully or partially handled through automated playbooks

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Count of incidents with automation involvement

Definition.DeepSeek: The total amount of analyst time saved through automation of repetitive tasks

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Estimated manual hours avoided through automation

Definition.DeepSeek: The calculated monetary cost of an incident, factoring in direct damages (device replacement, external experts) and indirect damages (productivity loss, brand impact)

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Direct costs + indirect costs per incident

Definition.DeepSeek: The total duration of business outages or worker downtime caused by security incidents

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Sum of outage durations per incident

Definition.DeepSeek: Comparison of actual financial losses against estimated losses that the SOC successfully prevented

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Estimated prevented losses - actual losses

Definition.DeepSeek: Evaluation of whether an incident occurred due to an unknown zero-day threat or a known vulnerability that could have been avoided with common security hygiene

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Percentage of incidents attributed to known vs. unknown vulnerabilities

Definition.DeepSeek: The proportion of incidents that resulted in measurable business disruption or impact

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: (Incidents with business impact / total incidents) × 100

Definition.DeepSeek: Evaluated through surveys or simulated cyber training exercises measuring analyst skills and knowledge

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Score from competency assessments or CTF exercises

Definition.DeepSeek: The percentage of SOC analysts who remain employed over a defined period

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: (Current staff - departures) / initial staff × 100

Definition.DeepSeek: The rate at which SOC analysts leave the organization and are replaced

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: (Number of departures / average total staff) × 100

Definition.DeepSeek: The percentage of analysts reporting symptoms of burnout or excessive workload stress

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: (Analysts reporting burnout / total analysts) × 100

Definition.DeepSeek: The proportion of analyst time exceeding normal capacity, indicating potential burnout risk

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: (Hours exceeding capacity / total hours) × 100

Definition.DeepSeek: The percentage of incident tickets that are reviewed by management for quality assurance purposes

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: (Tickets reviewed / total tickets) × 100

Definition.DeepSeek: Composite measure of SIEM effectiveness including accuracy of reporting, completeness and quality of information received, and quality of alerts issued to analysts

Formula_Elements.DeepSeek: Composite score across reporting accuracy, data completeness, alert quality

Description.DeepSeek: Tasks extracted from NICE Framework PR-CDA-001 and PR-CIR-001 are applicable to Tier 1 SOC Analysts, though frameworks do not specify tier levels

Rationale.DeepSeek: NICE Framework defines work roles (Cyber Defense Analyst, Cyber Defense Incident Responder) that align with SOC functions; industry sources confirm these roles encompass T1 responsibilities

Description.DeepSeek: O*NET 15-1212.00 (Information Security Analysts) tasks filtered for SOC monitoring, triage, detection, and response are representative of T1 responsibilities

Rationale.DeepSeek: O*NET does not distinguish SOC tiers; filtering applied based on task descriptions matching T1 scope

Description.DeepSeek: Theme taxonomy using NIST CSF functions (Detect/Respond) is appropriate for categorizing T1 SOC tasks

Rationale.DeepSeek: NIST CSF is authoritative framework for cybersecurity functions; Vielberth et al. (2020) and Agyepong (2023) use similar functional categorization

Description.DeepSeek: Domain mapping between metrics and tasks is valid when metric domain aligns with task theme, even without explicit source linkage

Rationale.DeepSeek: Phase 5 mapping rules explicitly define Domain mapping as moderately strict; allowed when metric defined for functional domain and tasks tagged to same domain

Description.DeepSeek: Metrics from academic and industry sources defined at SOC-level are applicable to T1 analyst performance measurement

Rationale.DeepSeek: T1 analysts contribute to SOC-level metrics; individual performance can be measured through contribution to aggregate metrics

Description.DeepSeek: Quality of Analysis (QoA) seven dimensions from Agyepong (2023) are appropriate for measuring T1 analyst performance

Rationale.DeepSeek: Agyepong (2023) PhD Thesis defines QoA for SOC analysts across all tiers; T1 analysts perform analysis, triage, and documentation tasks that align with QoA dimensions

Description.DeepSeek: Forrester altitude model (tactical/operational/strategic) classification provides valid framework for metric categorization

Rationale.DeepSeek: Forrester (2024) SOC Metrics altitude model is authoritative industry framework

Description.DeepSeek: Vielberth et al. (2020) SOC building blocks and role descriptions are valid for Tier 1 task extraction

Rationale.DeepSeek: Vielberth et al. (2020) IEEE Access paper explicitly describes Tier 1 responsibilities in SOC context

Description.DeepSeek: Radiant Security, Q-Sec, and Wiz (2025) industry role explainers accurately represent Tier 1 SOC Analyst responsibilities

Rationale.DeepSeek: Sources explicitly state 'Tier 1' or 'Level 1' in role descriptions

Description.DeepSeek: Tasks with context SOC-T1 are appropriate for Tier 1 analyst role; tasks flagged SOC-GENERAL require human judgment

Rationale.DeepSeek: Phase 2 protocol required flagging ambiguous tasks for human review

Description.DeepSeek: Evidence_Link field with citation string is sufficient for traceability in absence of direct URLs

Rationale.DeepSeek: Some sources (NICE Framework, academic papers) do not have stable URLs for specific sections

Description.DeepSeek: Task descriptions marked [summarised] maintain semantic fidelity to source while avoiding copyright issues

Rationale.DeepSeek: Phase 2 protocol permitted neutral one-line summaries for copyright-sensitive sources

Description.ChatGPT: 12 tasks have no metrics mapped
Description.DeepSeek: No source explicitly links QoA (Quality of Analysis) dimensions to specific T1 tasks; mapping required domain inference based on task descriptions
Description.Gemini: Missing per-task effectiveness metrics for manual log review and vulnerability review.
Description.Grok: 9 metrics (MET-010 to MET-027) have no Task_ID mappings

Rationale.Gemini: Authoritative literature focuses heavily on the reactive alert lifecycle (MTTA/MTTT), leaving proactive or manual discovery tasks unmeasured in technical terms.

Suggested_Future_Work.Gemini: Develop specific 'Validation Accuracy' or 'Discovery Rate' metrics for non-alert-driven human activities.

Description.ChatGPT: Some industry metrics lacked formula elements
Description.DeepSeek: Alert volume (MET-037) and analyst workload (MET-040) metrics lack explicit linkage to specific T1 tasks in industry sources
Description.Gemini: NICE Task Statements for Cyber Defense Analyst bleed into Tier 2 forensic investigation.
Description.Grok: TSK-008 ("Implement security measures") is the broadest ONET task

Rationale.Gemini: Broad framework definitions in NICE do not always align with the functional 'Tier 1' ceiling observed in industry SOC operational tiers.

Suggested_Future_Work.Gemini: Map tasks to a 'Decision-Logic Complexity' scale to isolate Tier 1 definitively from more advanced analytical roles.

Description.ChatGPT: 5 tasks flagged SOC-GENERAL, unclear if Tier 1
Description.DeepSeek: MTTR (Mean Time to Respond) has multiple definitions across sources: NIST defines as time from detection to containment; Splunk defines as time from detection to resolution; Forsberg & Frantti define separate MTTR (reaction) metric
Description.Gemini: Assumption that all Tier 1 tasks contribute equally to analyst burnout.
Description.Grok: All 16 tasks assumed SOC-T1 (no SOC-GENERAL flags)

Rationale.Gemini: A domain-mapping was applied for burnout across all tasks, ignoring that cognitive load varies significantly between routine documentation and high-pressure triage.

Suggested_Future_Work.Gemini: Conduct AHP weighting to determine the actual cognitive load per specific Task_ID to refine burnout modeling.

Description.ChatGPT: Certain tasks are high-level (e.g., 'monitor alerts')
Description.DeepSeek: False Positive Rate definition varies: some sources calculate per alert, others per incident; threshold for 'false positive' classification varies
Description.Gemini: High degree of subjectivity in qualitative 'Quality' metrics (QoA/QIR).
Description.Grok: QoA (MET-007) treated as directly mappable to analysis/triage tasks

Rationale.Gemini: Literature identifying quality dimensions (Agyepong) lacks a universal, tool-agnostic rubric for consistent scoring across different SOC environments.

Suggested_Future_Work.Gemini: Standardize a tool-agnostic 7-dimension QoA rubric suitable for automated scoring integration.

Description.ChatGPT: Some metrics lack Human-Factor dimension assignment
Description.DeepSeek: Differentiation between SOC-T1 and SOC-GENERAL tasks required human judgment; 13 tasks removed in Phase 2 revision based on human review
Description.Gemini: Lack of metrics for 'Anomaly Identification' that occurs before an automated alert is triggered.
Description.Grok: Technical Performance Metrics Framework (MET-026) remains abstract (4 novel metrics not enumerated)

Rationale.Gemini: Authoritative sources do not provide formulas for human-initiated discovery that happens outside the context of SIEM/IDS signaling.

Suggested_Future_Work.Gemini: Propose 'Human-Initiated Detection Rate' (HIDR) as a new effectiveness KPI for elite analyst discovery.

Description.ChatGPT: Certain metrics labeled SOC-level not analyst-level
Description.DeepSeek: Human capital metrics (MET-062 through MET-066) lack explicit linkage to T1 tasks; mapped via organizational context rather than task-level association
Description.Grok: No explicit source link between alert volume (MET-009) and every monitoring task

Description.DeepSeek: Business impact metrics (MET-057 through MET-061) defined in sources at organizational level; T1 contribution difficult to isolate
Description.Grok: NIST CSF Recover function has zero tasks/metrics

Description.DeepSeek: Theme definitions required judgment to establish exclusion boundaries; some tasks could plausibly fit multiple themes
Description.Grok: 100% task coverage achieved; 33% of master metrics unmapped
